behavior_summary: This script implements an NPC guard (U8P_GUARD8) with: a simple U8P_GUARD8::look/inspect response, fast-area entry behavior that revives/relocates the NPC when found dead, a scheduled respawn/reset routine, and a multi-branch conversation handler with a menu-driven Q&A that updates persistent world flags.

U8P_GUARD8::look: when the player examines the NPC it checks Npc::isDead(this) and barks either a dead description ('dead guardsman') or a living one ('guardsman'), then suspends — a short U8P_GUARD8::look/inspect handler.

U8P_GUARD8::enterFastArea: triggered on fast-area entry; if the NPC is dead it clears dead and in-combat flags (Npc::clrDead, Npc::clrInCombat) and teleports the NPC to fixed coordinates (revive/relocate). If alive it invokes behavior/AI helpers (METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7) to resume normal activity.

U8P_GUARD8::schedule: sets timing/state temporaries, calls a scheduling helper (METHOD::func143A), waits; if process_result() returns true it clears the dead state (Npc::clrDead), calls a post-U8P_GUARD8::schedule helper (METHOD::func133F) and suspends — this appears to manage timed respawn/reset.

U8P_GUARD8::use: conversation entrypoint. It prepares transient spawn/free data, counts several global 'area cleared' flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) to possibly trigger an immediate urgent warning about Titans, otherwise runs greeting branches (first-meet vs repeat, angry vs normal, uses a small urandom-based variance), builds a menu (local2), presents Ask to capture the player's choice (local1), removes asked options (MenuRemove) and sets/clears flags (guard8Met, guard8Pissed, guard8Thanked) accordingly before ending the conversation.
notable_control_flow: Look/inspect: single conditional on Npc::isDead(this) -> two short barks then suspend.
Fast-area entry: conditional path: dead -> revive/teleport -> suspend; alive -> call METHOD::func091C -> suspend -> call METHOD::func15E7 -> suspend.
Schedule: initialize locals -> METHOD::func143A -> suspend/wait -> if process_result() true -> Npc::clrDead + METHOD::func133F -> suspend.

Conversation (U8P_GUARD8::use): early bailout if counter (local3) high due to hydrosFree/hasHeart/pyrosFree -> urgent warning and end. Otherwise branching by guard8Met and guard8Pissed for greeting, randomized variant via urandom, then a conversation loop: Ask menu (local2) -> capture choice (local1) -> respond and MenuRemove used to prevent repeating options -> possibly set guard8Met/guard8Pissed/guard8Thanked -> end conversation.
open_questions: Exact responsibilities of METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F are unclear — they appear to be internal AI/scheduling helpers but their concrete actions are not shown.
What external event or engine condition starts U8P_GUARD8::schedule (periodic timer, death event, or other)?
Precise semantics and intended values of U8P_GUARD8::schedule() local temporaries (local0..local3) and mapping to real-world timing or spawn tables are inferred but not certain.
The transient FREE::func1C19 and FREE::resetRef usage in U8P_GUARD8::use appears to manipulate spawn/free references for dialogue setup; the exact data mutated is unknown.
When and why guard8Pissed is set vs cleared in all branches is partly inferred from menu branches but some transitions remain ambiguous.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD8::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a concise response when the player inspects the guardsman: report 'dead guardsman' if dead, otherwise 'guardsman', then end the U8P_GUARD8::look handling.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player inspects or 'looks at' the NPC (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::look
function_map.role: Player-facing inspect handler that checks life state and emits a short bark

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area trigger handler that revives/relocates dead NPCs or resumes AI for alive NPCs

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::schedule
function_map.role: Timed/scheduled respawn or reset manager that waits and finalizes revival on success

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::use
function_map.role: Conversation/dialogue handler: builds a menu, branches greetings, removes asked options, and updates global dialogue flags

function_map.name: METHOD::func091C
function_map.role: Internal behavior/AI helper invoked from U8P_GUARD8::enterFastArea; exact purpose inferred as movement/AI resume

function_map.name: METHOD::func15E7
function_map.role: Follow-up internal behavior helper invoked from U8P_GUARD8::enterFastArea; inferred continuation of AI

function_map.name: METHOD::func143A
function_map.role: Scheduling helper invoked by U8P_GUARD8::schedule(); likely starts/configures a timed wait

function_map.name: METHOD::func133F
function_map.role: Post-U8P_GUARD8::schedule helper invoked after process_result() to finalize respawn/reset

external_calls.purpose: Check NPC life-state to choose which short bark to play
Decide whether to run revive/teleport branch or resume-AI branch
Clear dead flag to revive NPC on area-entry when dead
Move NPC to fixed map/coordinates as part of revive/relocate
Invoke internal AI/behavior routine when NPC is alive
Invoke follow-up AI/behavior routine after func091C
Start/configure scheduled wait or timer using local timing/state values
Check boolean outcome of the awaited scheduled process to decide follow-up actions
Clear dead flag to finalize revival when scheduled wait reports success
Finalize post-U8P_GUARD8::schedule actions following a successful wait
Transient spawn/free query or setup before starting conversation (exact data unclear)
Reset a spawn/free reference (refId, value) during conversation setup
Randomly vary greeting text to avoid repetitive repeat-dialogue

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Indicator that increments a local counter used to decide urgent Titan-warning dialogue

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Indicator that increments the same local counter for urgent-warning branching

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Indicator that increments the counter used for urgent-warning branching

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Determines location/regime-specific dialogue wording and whether guard8Thanked can be set

reads.name: guard8Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to pick first-meeting vs repeat greeting paths

reads.name: guard8Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to select an angry greeting path when true

writes.name: guard8Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to mark that the player has met this guard and suppress first-meet text on subsequent talks

writes.name: guard8Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set or cleared based on conversation choices to track the guard's annoyance state

writes.name: guard8Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Conditionally set (when devonInRule is true and not already set) to record a one-time thank-you acknowledgement

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements an NPC guard (U8P_GUARD8) with: a simple U8P_GUARD8::look/inspect response, fast-area entry behavior that revives/relocates the NPC when found dead, a scheduled respawn/reset routine, and a multi-branch conversation handler with a menu-driven Q&A that updates persistent world flags.
